instructions:
Run the existing link checker scoped to the target files and fix every broken internal link found.

Steps:

1. Run `python3 tools/check_internal_links.py` (the script scans the whole repo; filter the report to entries in the target_files list).
2. For each broken link in a target file:
   a. Identify the intended destination. The most common causes are: (i) the destination page was moved/renamed — find the new path by grep; (ii) the link uses an old slug — check docs.json for the canonical slug; (iii) the link points to an anchor that no longer exists — check the destination file's headings.
   b. Update the link to the correct path. Use repo-absolute paths starting with `/` for cross-section links; relative paths for intra-section links.
   c. If you cannot confidently identify the intended destination, do NOT guess — instead, drop the link (keep the link text as plain text) and add a note in your result describing the dropped link.
3. Do NOT modify any body content beyond the link itself.
4. Do NOT touch links to external URLs (http/https/mailto).

Report the per-file count of links fixed and any links dropped in the result's `notes` field.

validators.name: no_broken_internal_links_in_targets
validators.command: python3 tools/check_internal_links.py 2>&1 | grep -E 'social-plus-sdk/getting-started/' | grep -i 'missing\|broken' || echo OK_NO_BROKEN_LINKS_IN_TARGETS
